capability_objective: From the long vehicle fixture, resolve “Shuttle 30” in Depot D, status active. Start with a scoped search; do not guess an ID from the surrounding records. After search identifies the record, fetch its full details by that returned ID. Ignore same-name records in other locations and slightly different names in Depot D. Make no persistent changes. Finish with one sentence beginning “Found:” and include the stable ID.
prompt:
Work only in the supplied sandbox. Resolve the requested vehicle with the genuine local `./fleetctl` executable through the harness shell tool, then create `vehicle-resolution.txt`. Inspect the executable's top-level built-in help with `./fleetctl --help` before accessing fleet data. Do not inspect the executable source, protected files, setup code, runtime database, or execution journal to obtain vehicle data, and do not write a wrapper, script, function, or loop to perform the lookup workflow.

Find the vehicle whose exact name is `Shuttle 30` and whose exact location is `Depot D`; its required status is `active`. Your first fleet-data operation must be one scoped search using both the exact name and exact location. Wait for that result. Treat same-name vehicles at other locations and every slightly different name in Depot D as distractors. Only if the returned search records contain exactly one qualifying exact-name, exact-location, active vehicle, take the stable ID from that returned match and use it in a separate, later shell-tool action to retrieve the full record. Do not retrieve any other ID, guess an ID, derive it from fixture order, or take deliverable values from a search summary or backing file.

This is a strictly read-only resolution. Do not invoke collection listing, profile, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify operations, and do not alter vehicle, profile, availability, or notification state.

`vehicle-resolution.txt` must contain exactly one UTF-8 line, terminated by a newline, in this shape:
`Found: <name> (<stable ID>) at <location>; status <status>; date <date>.`
Replace every angle-bracket placeholder only with the corresponding value from the successful full-record retrieval. Do not mention or copy any distractor into the deliverable.

If the scoped search is missing or ambiguous, do not perform a detail retrieval and do not guess; report that the vehicle could not be uniquely resolved. Otherwise, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` after writing the file. Leave the executable, protected files, and setup code unchanged, and do not inspect or manually edit tool-managed runtime state or the journal. Finish your user-facing response with the same single `Found:` sentence and no other prose.
